deities.name: Dinistr
deities.desire: Destroy and toy with Material denizens

deities.name: Felujitas
deities.desire: Fix and rejuvenate what is broken

deities.name: Twyll
deities.desire: Make others feel foolish through deception

deities.name: Forgotten Obsession
deities.desire: Make things the right way with nothing out of line

deities.name: Vonalzo
deities.desire: Win through power, supremacy, and conquest

deities.name: Fajdalom
deities.desire: Torture denizens of the Material Plane

deities.name: Mega-Terozash
deities.desire: Return to the original Primordial Unity

deities.name: Ataliad
deities.desire: Keep things the same, unmoving, unchanging

deities.name: Uralom
deities.desire: Make the strongest champions and defeat them

deities.name: Divine Seed and Superego Destruction Mechanism

factions.name: House Panim
factions.summary: Divine mediation and afterlife-preparation house whose services bind religion, status, and commerce together.
factions.motive: Preserve indispensable control over afterlife preparation and god-facing mediation.

factions.name: House Shirsh
factions.summary: Magic-investigative house tied to the Magi Magistratus and celestial-mystical study.
factions.motive: Keep magical misconduct legible and maintain Shirsh authority over sensitive arcane inquiry.

factions.name: Roadwardens Order
factions.summary: The largest peacekeeping army spread through the empire as road police, securing trade routes and public order under She
factions.motive: Keep roads safe, commerce moving, and Shelk’s authority visibly credible.

factions.name: Red Hood Guild
factions.summary: Elusive independent guild operating in shadowed relation to trade, intrigue, and forbidden or covert work.
factions.motive: Exploit the gaps created by rigid law, secret demand, and elite hypocrisy.

factions.name: Shadowhands
factions.summary: Covert branch of ORE specialized in corruption, contraband, and illicit magic investigations.
factions.motive: Neutralize hidden threats before they become public destabilizers.

factions.name: Communal Ration Allocators
factions.summary: Sheresh quartermasters and ration judges who keep dome survival stores, heat budgets, and emergency reserves balanced.
factions.motive: Prevent scarcity cascades by keeping heat, food, and repair materials distributed according to survi

factions.name: Forge Dedication Halls
factions.summary: Ceremonial forge communities where craft, oath, and renewal are bound together under public House supervision.
factions.motive: Keep forge labor sacred, socially legible, and politically loyal to the rebuilding order.

factions.name: Artificers’ Guild
factions.type: Soreheim relic-technology and magical integration faction
factions.summary: Alliance artificers tasked with integrating relics and rare controlled magic into industry under strict oversight.
factions.motive: Convert rare magical knowledge into durable Alliance advantage without allowing uncontrolled arcane

factions.name: Guild Sanction Board
factions.summary: Union-facing sanction authority that disciplines guild misbehavior, contract breaches, and destabilizing market conduct.
factions.motive: Preserve network trust by proving that commercial power remains governable.

factions.name: Frontier Hammer Companies
factions.summary: Mixed crews that defend, repair, and push dangerous industrial frontiers in the Eternal Lands.
factions.motive: Secure extraction zones and keep remote infrastructure alive in lands where predators and geothermal

factions.name: Engineers’ Consortium
factions.summary: Primary infrastructure planners and maintainers for Titan Towers, spans, depots, and industrial systems.
factions.motive: Keep the Alliance physically functional and expansion-capable through disciplined engineering.

factions.name: Loss Ledger Circles
factions.summary: Sheresh circles that record deaths, disappearances, and irrecoverable losses so survival decisions remain socially remem
factions.motive: Prevent the harsh arithmetic of survival from erasing grief, accountability, or cautionary memory.

factions.name: Agricultural Syndicate
factions.summary: Alliance-wide coordinators of communal food production, irrigation discipline, and harvest distribution across the Unter
factions.motive: Maintain food stability, preserve productive land, and keep communal supply obligations met.

factions.name: Iron Accord
factions.summary: Roazian coalition of military architects and engineers responsible for fortifications, strategic facilities, and antimag
factions.motive: Keep Roaz defensible, logistically efficient, and superior in managing physical and arcane threats.

factions.name: Office of Roazian Enforcement
factions.summary: The main law-and-order arm of Roaz, responsible for anti-magic enforcement, trade security, prisoner management, and jud
factions.motive: Preserve lawful order and state control over risk, especially magical risk.

factions.name: House Mimolot
factions.summary: Scholarly-magic house centered on education, book tariff systems, and controlled magical production.
factions.motive: Maintain educational authority and magical legitimacy while controlling access to dangerous knowledg

factions.name: House Cosmouth
factions.summary: Maritime archival and shipbuilding house anchoring Cosmouth’s trade, recordkeeping, and ocean-facing power.
factions.motive: Preserve maritime influence, archive legitimacy, and shipwright strength within the Principalities.

factions.name: Magi Magistratus
factions.summary: Magical law investigators and enforcers operating across houses to pursue misconduct and arcane irregularity.
factions.motive: Keep dangerous or illicit magic within legible boundaries and preserve Shirsh authority over magical

factions.name: House Zootia
factions.summary: Raw-material and agricultural house supplying wool, crops, livestock, and other essential inputs to the Principalities.
factions.motive: Remain indispensable by feeding and supplying the broader house network.

factions.name: House Shelk
factions.summary: Road-policing, fashion-patronage, and harmony-centered noble house governing Shelk’s cultural power.
factions.motive: Keep trade secure, fashion prestigious, and Shelk visibly harmonious without surrendering political

factions.name: Descent Charter Compacts
factions.summary: Interlocking charters governing who may enter the Scarlet Depths, what is owed after descent, and how recovered material
factions.motive: Keep underworld extraction productive without letting unsanctioned descent destabilize House order.

factions.name: House Roaz
factions.summary: Legal-industrial noble house anchoring strict enforcement, quarry extraction, and strategic antimagic development.
factions.motive: Preserve order, expand strategic strength, and ensure magic never outruns state control.

factions.name: Route Warden Compacts
factions.summary: Sheresh cross-commune agreements governing launch windows, escorts, and loss reporting between domes.
factions.motive: Keep necessary travel survivable and socially accountable in a lethal environment.

factions.name: Rebuilding Oath Courts
factions.summary: Public oath forums where rebuilding obligations, restitution, and civic renewal promises are witnessed and judged.
factions.motive: Turn destruction into accountable renewal instead of endless vengeance or neglect.

factions.name: Great House Citadel Circle
factions.summary: The public ring of matriarchal Great Houses whose citadels define status, rivalries, and production power on Psanan.
factions.motive: Preserve House precedence while extracting strength, loyalty, and legitimacy from volcanic endurance

factions.name: Giant Council
factions.summary: Storm Giant-led supreme governing body of the Soreheim Alliance, advised by Decon Von Reckshem.
factions.motive: Drive progress, enforce communal hierarchy, and keep the Alliance expanding under giant authority.

factions.name: Ash Port Factors
factions.summary: Harbor-facing brokers who move volcanic exports, ration incoming necessities, and negotiate ash-window shipping from Psa
factions.motive: Keep Psanan’s dangerous export economy functioning despite ashfall, heat, and route volatility.

factions.name: Miners’ Assembly
factions.summary: The principal extraction organization for the Alliance’s mining zones, especially in the Eternal Lands.
factions.motive: Keep ore, crystal, and geothermal resource streams flowing without collapsing the frontier.

factions.name: Fashion Artisans Collective
factions.summary: House Shelk-aligned fashion network that organizes designers, workshops, apprentices, and cultural showcases.
factions.motive: Preserve Shelk’s artistic prestige while turning fashion into durable political and economic power.

factions.name: Containment Research Concord
factions.summary: Sheresh research bloc coordinating contamination measurement, shield experimentation, and controlled study of dangerous
factions.motive: Understand and survive magical contamination without letting inquiry endanger the domes.

factions.name: House Mal Remnant Claimants
factions.summary: Residual Mal-aligned claimants who preserve debt, title, or memory claims after the Union successor transition.
factions.motive: Recover lost standing, settle old obligations in their favor, or resist total absorption into the Un

factions.name: The Union
factions.summary: Guild-backed successor order replacing Mal’s active trade-mediation role across the former Mal sphere.
factions.motive: Keep trade moving, disputes arbitrated, and guild power coordinated without returning to fallen-hous

factions.name: Sheresh Dome Stewards
factions.summary: Steward-administrators who keep dome life functioning through maintenance law, heat discipline, and communal survival pr
factions.motive: Keep domes alive first, comfortable second, and politically stable throughout crisis.

norms: Survival-aware greetings often include weather, dome status, or route condition before personal matters.
rumors: People say something in Aurora Crown Commune has grown more tense than the officials admit.
Merchants claim the last busy cycle in Aurora Crown Commune left someone unpaid or publicly embarrassed.

hooks.where_strangers_gather: At the most practical public node: inn bench, queue court, market edge, dock gate, shrine steps, or freight lane.
hooks.public_notice_drawing_attention: A new notice in Aurora Crown Commune is drawing comment because it changes someone’s movement, cost, or safety.
hooks.visible_tension: People in Aurora Crown Commune are acting as though a pressure everyone knows about has become harder to ignore.
hooks.private_trouble: Someone respectable in Aurora Crown Commune is privately entangled in an obligation they cannot publicly explain.
hooks.one_hour_entanglement: A newcomer in Aurora Crown Commune could be pulled into a dispute, delivery problem, witness request, or rumor test within an hour of arrival.

polities.name: House Panim
polities.summary: Half-Elves remain primary, with Humans and Elves retained as the most coherent secondary populations for a central religious and afterlife-mediating p

polities.name: House Shirsh
polities.summary: Half-Elves remain primary, while Elves, Humans, and Gnomes support its magical-investigative and administrative posture.

polities.name: Psanan
polities.summary: Volcanic island polity of matriarchal Great Houses, forge industry, underworld exploration, endurance custom, and cyclic renewal under harsh ash and h

polities.name: House Mal
polities.summary: Former trade-and-banking principality preserved as a fallen predecessor whose infrastructure, claims, and memory still shape the Union successor heart

polities.name: Sheresh Communes
polities.summary: Humans, Elves, and Halflings are resolved as the core, with Half-Elves retained as a conservative adjacency-based secondary race.

polities.name: House Mimolot
polities.summary: Mimolot is explicitly reconciled as a mixed polity in which Elves, Humans, and Gnomes all hold stable demographic presence under differentiated functi

polities.name: House Cosmouth
polities.summary: Humans remain dominant while Tritons and Tortles are retained as the cleanest maritime secondary races for a floating, sea-linked shipwright state.

polities.name: House Zootia
polities.summary: Yuan-Ti remain the defining race, with Humans, Half-Elves, and Elves preserved as secondary populations through labor, trade, and imperial integration

polities.name: Union Successor Heartland
polities.summary: The post-Mal internal region whose main population areas are highly industrialized and urban, where guild courts, converted banking districts, warehou

polities.name: Principality of Roaz
polities.summary: Humans remain primary, while Half-Elves, Elves, and Gnomes align with Roazian law, industry, and anti-magic technical culture.

polities.name: Soreheim Alliance
polities.summary: Giant-led alliance ruled from Soreheim Proper, where central quota authority, industrial hierarchy, and regional mediation hold together a large multi

polities.name: Principality of Shelk
polities.summary: Elves remain primary, with Half-Elves, Humans, Gnomes, and Firbolgs retained as the best-supported extension of Shelk’s urban, artistic, and nature-li

polities.name: Nomdara Caravan
polities.summary: A mobile connective polity organized as a colossal caravan-city on wheels, with trade expeditions, council-of-elders governance, consensus procedure,

polities.name: The Union
polities.summary: Humans and Gnomes remain primary; Half-Elves, Elves, and Yuan-Ti are formalized as durable resident blocs in the exchange and mediation core.

polities.name: The Principalities
polities.summary: Imperial umbrella polity of noble territorial powers, imperial central authority, and a successor trade order centered on The Union, now carrying an e
